metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry capturing the validity-on-read decision (compute_validity replaces retire_attested_pin mutation) so future gate implementers understand the system's consistency contract.
findings.finding: The shift from write-time mutation (retire_attested_pin) to validity-on-read (compute_validity) is a novel architectural decision — changing the system's consistency model for reopen invalidation — but no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry is mentioned in the plan.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope — no documentation section present
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short ADR (or a section in CLAUDE.md/ARCHITECTURE.md) titled 'Reopen invalidation: validity-on-read over mutation' that records: the old approach, why it had a gap, and the invariant that gates MUST call compute_validity rather than mutate attestation records.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the `completion.py` child-closure path (`_child_closure_findings`, line 94) is migrated to pass `kind='completion-verifier'` to `verify_signature` AND is routed through `compute_validity`, not left as a bare no-kind `rebar.verify_signature(cid).get('verdict')` call — specify the exact call-site API shape in the plan or AC.
findings.finding: The child-closure path in completion.py (`_child_closure_findings`) calls `rebar.verify_signature(cid, repo_root=repo_root).get('verdict')` with no `kind=` argument (completion.py:94). After cc1b lands, `verify_signature` will default to `kind='plan-review'`, so the child-closure check will silently verify the wrong attestation kind on tickets that carry both kinds in the `attestations` map. The plan's AC says 'close + child-closure verify+validate completion-verifier' but the mechanism description does not specify how this call site, which uses the `rebar` public API rather than `signing.verify_signature`, receives the `kind='completion-verifier'` parameter and is routed through `compute_validity`.
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC or Scope section, add: '_child_closure_findings in completion.py migrates from `rebar.verify_signature(cid).get("verdict") != "certified"` to `compute_validity(signing.verify_signature(cid, kind="completion-verifier"), state, "completion-verifier")` — requires that the public `rebar.verify_signature` API also accepts and passes through `kind=` per cc1b.' Ensure cc1b's `kind=` parameter is plumbed through the `rebar` facade (rebar/__init__.py or equivalent) so `_child_closure_findings` can call it with the correct kind.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring at least one end-to-end (non-mocked) test for each migrated gate path (claim gate, close gate, child-closure) that reads from a real `attestations[kind]` map and confirms the gate decision matches the expected outcome for both the valid and invalid attestation cases.
findings.finding: The plan migrates ALL gate checks (claim, close, child-closure) from the legacy `verify_signature` path to `verify + compute_validity` reading `attestations[kind]` — a full cutover that makes the new path the default — but no acceptance criterion or testing section requires these gates to be exercised end-to-end (live, with a real attestations map). Offline/mock-based tests could go green while the `attestations[kind]` lookup is silently broken.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Claim gate verifies+validates plan-review; close + child-closure verify+validate completion-verifier, reading attestations[kind]'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Covered by an integration test that writes a real attestation into `attestations[kind]`, runs the gate, and asserts the correct allow/reject decision — no mocking of verify or compute_validity.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or CLAUDE.md/architecture.md section) capturing the validity-on-read decision — that gates MUST call `compute_validity` rather than mutate attestation records on reopen — so the invariant is discoverable alongside the existing ADR series.
findings.finding: The shift from write-time mutation (retire_attested_pin) to validity-on-read (compute_validity) is a novel system-level consistency contract change for this codebase, but the plan names no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry to document it — despite the project maintaining an active ADR series (0001-0008) that records exactly this class of 'why we chose this consistency model' decision (e.g., ADR 0002 for code-drift invalidation, ADR 0004 for reconciler snapshot contract).
findings.location: ## What — 'PAIRED removal (no gap)' rationale; ## Scope — no documentation file listed
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/0009-validity-on-read-reopen-invalidation.md` (following the existing ADR format) recording: old approach (retire_attested_pin mutation), the gap it had (best-effort, could miss), the new invariant (compute_validity checks signed_at >= last_reopened_at from compiled state), and the rule that no transition may mutate an attestation record.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `kind` vocabulary ('plan-review', 'completion-verifier') as shared constants (or a small enum) in a single location that `compute_validity`'s dispatch and the gate callers all import from, rather than leaving the implementer to reconcile two module-local private constants.
findings.finding: The `kind` parameter of `compute_validity` dispatches per-kind validity logic using string values ('plan-review', 'completion-verifier'), but the plan never defines these as a shared enum/constant set. The authoritative strings are currently split across two module-local constants (`_MANIFEST_PREFIX = 'plan-review'` in attest.py and `_REVIEWER_ID = 'completion-verifier'` in completion.py) with no shared vocabulary that `compute_validity`'s dispatch can canonically import.
findings.location: What section / AC1: `compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind, *, repo_root=None)`
findings.suggested_fix: Add `PLAN_REVIEW_KIND = 'plan-review'` and `COMPLETION_VERIFIER_KIND = 'completion-verifier'` to `src/rebar/signing.py` (or a new `src/rebar/llm/attestation_kinds.py`) and have attest.py, completion.py, and compute_validity all import from there.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how `compute_validity` checks 'material-unchanged-since signed_at' for completion-verifier: either (a) add a `material: <fingerprint>` line to `_verdict_manifest` in transition.py (and state this as an in-scope change), or (b) explicitly remove this condition from AC3 for the completion-verifier kind and describe the actual completion-specific staleness rule.
findings.finding: AC3 requires `completion valid = ... AND material-unchanged-since signed_at`, but the existing `_verdict_manifest` for completion-verifier (transition.py:282-305) does not record a material fingerprint in the manifest — it only records `completion-verifier: PASS`, `ticket:`, `model:`, `runner:`, and optionally `verified-at-sha:`. `compute_validity` therefore has no in-manifest source for the material at signing time, making the 'material-unchanged-since' check under-specified as to where the baseline material comes from.
findings.location: AC3: 'completion valid = certified AND closed AND not-reopened-since AND material-unchanged-since signed_at' / src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282-305 `_verdict_manifest`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope or What section whether compute_validity belongs in plan_review/attest.py as a shared utility (and completion.py imports it from there) or whether it should live in a shared verification module; state the rationale.
findings.finding: The 'What' section locates compute_validity in 'the verification layer' but the 'Scope' section pins it specifically to `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`, a gate-specific module; the plan does not explain why a function that also drives the completion-verifier gate belongs in the plan-review module, creating a cross-section tension an implementer must silently resolve.
findings.location: What section ('in the verification layer') vs. Scope section ('src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add ACs for: (a) deleting/updating existing tests for `retire_attested_pin`, and (b) adding a unit test for `compute_validity` that asserts `valid=False, reason` mentions reopened when `signed_at < last_reopened_at`.
findings.finding: `retire_attested_pin` (signing.py) and its reopen call (transition.py) are being deleted, and their behavioral responsibility is transferred to `compute_validity`'s `signed_at >= last_reopened_at` check, but the plan mentions no work to (a) remove or update existing tests for `retire_attested_pin`, or (b) add tests confirming that the new reopen-invalidation path in `compute_validity` covers the same cases.
findings.location: Scope — 'src/rebar/signing.py (remove retire_attested_pin)'; AC5 — 'retire_attested_pin + its reopen call removed IN THIS SLICE'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify (in AC or Scope) how gates handle a missing attestations[kind] entry — returning a structured {valid: false, reason: 'no attestation for <kind>'} rather than raising a KeyError or passing None to compute_validity.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify what happens when attestations[kind] is absent in the map (i.e., the gate is checked before any attestation of that kind has ever been written) — consumers may receive a KeyError or an unhandled None rather than a clean 'not attested' failure reason.
findings.location: AC line: 'Claim gate verifies+validates plan-review; close + child-closure verify+validate completion-verifier, reading attestations[kind]'
findings.suggested_fix: In the gate call-site pseudocode, add: `attestation = ticket_state.get('attestations', {}).get(kind)` and short-circuit to `{valid: False, reason: f'no {kind} attestation found'}` before calling verify/compute_validity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what `compute_validity`'s callers pass (and what `compute_validity` returns) when `attestations[kind]` is absent — either document the None-attestation contract explicitly ('compute_validity({}, ...)` returns `{valid: False, reason: "unsigned"}`) or state that the caller must apply cc1b's legacy fallback before invoking compute_validity.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for `compute_validity` (or the migrated gate callers) when `attestations[kind]` is absent for a given kind — for example, when the close gate reads `attestations['completion-verifier']` on a ticket signed before cc1b's map is populated. cc1b's AC states 'legacy `signature` fallback when the map is absent' for `verify_signature`, but `compute_validity` is a separate layer the plan doesn't connect to this fallback rule.
findings.location: What section / AC2: 'Claim gate verifies+validates plan-review; close + child-closure verify+validate completion-verifier, reading attestations[kind]'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before removing `retire_attested_pin`, enumerate ALL call sites (grep/AST search across the repo) and explicitly confirm `transition.py` is the only caller, or list and handle the others.
findings.finding: The plan removes `retire_attested_pin` from `signing.py` but only names `transition.py` as its call site. If other call-sites exist (e.g., tests, other commands), removing the function silently breaks them without acknowledgment.
findings.location: What section / 'PAIRED removal' paragraph; Scope: signing.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line note to the Scope section: 'Confirmed only call site: transition.py:reopen (verified by grep retire_attested_pin).' If other call sites exist, enumerate them as explicit removals.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (e.g., a feature flag, an expand-contract sequence where the old `retire_attested_pin` path is kept behind a flag until `compute_validity` is validated in production, or a documented revert procedure) and specify the fallback behavior when `attestations[kind]` is absent on a pre-migration ticket.
findings.finding: The plan explicitly justifies removing `retire_attested_pin` and its reopen call as a paired, irreversible removal, but provides no rollback/back-out plan if `compute_validity`'s reopen-awareness check is buggy or if in-flight tickets (pre-114f migration) lack `attestations[kind]`, leaving no recovery path without re-adding the mutation.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section 'Rollback' stating: (1) the feature can be back-ported by reverting this slice without data loss since no records are mutated; (2) `compute_validity` should treat a missing `attestations[kind]` as `{"valid": False, "reason": "no attestation found"}` (fail-closed) to handle pre-114f tickets gracefully, rather than raising a KeyError.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that specifies a concrete validation mechanism, e.g., 'an integration test (or CI gate) shows claim/close commands succeed on a valid attestation and are rejected after a reopen, without any record mutation occurring.'
findings.finding: No AC or validation mechanism provides a concrete before/after or operational signal that the gate behavior is correct after migration; all five ACs describe internal system properties (function signatures, data-field reads, boolean logic) with no observable workflow comparison or test-outcome gate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC#4 to name the exact existing freshness check (e.g., 'material field matches `orchestrator.material_fingerprint(repo_root)` AND no code-drift files have changed since `signed_at`') so the criterion is verifiable without reverse-engineering existing code.
findings.finding: AC#4's 'existing freshness' is under-specified as a success criterion — it names the conditions parenthetically ('material + filesystem code-drift') but does not state what constitutes a pass or how an agent verifies the existing logic was correctly preserved vs. changed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC#4: 'plan-review valid = existing freshness (material + filesystem code-drift) AND not-reopened-since'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'existing freshness (material + filesystem code-drift)' with a concrete statement such as: 'material field equals `orchestrator.material_fingerprint(repo_root)` AND no tracked code files under `repo_root` have a mtime newer than `signed_at`' — matching whatever the current attest.py check does, quoted verbatim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit implementation sequence within the slice: (1) implement + unit-test `compute_validity` in isolation, (2) migrate `claim_gate_check` and close/child-closure gates to use it, (3) remove `retire_attested_pin` and its call only after gate tests pass — all committed together but clearly ordered.
findings.finding: The plan bundles four file changes across 3+ layers into a single undifferentiated slice with no internal sequencing or de-risking order; there is no thin vertical sub-step (e.g., 'implement + test compute_validity in isolation, then migrate gates, then remove retire_attested_pin') to surface integration failures early.
findings.location: What / Scope sections; no sequencing sub-steps present

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in § Why or § What explaining WHY `compute_validity` is a separate function from `verify_record` (e.g. crypto/lifecycle separation of concerns, independent testability of HMAC certification vs. business-validity rules, avoiding coupling ticket_state reads into the pure crypto layer).
findings.finding: The plan states a positive functional goal ('reopen must invalidate WITHOUT mutating records') but does not articulate a positive rationale for the specific structural choice of `compute_validity` as a NEW SEPARATE function over the alternative of enriching `verify_record`/`verify_signature` directly with the lifecycle/freshness layer. The absence of a stated positive rationale for the chosen interface boundary (separate validator vs. enriched verifier) leaves implementers without design intent guidance if they encounter pressure to simplify by inlining.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
